content.problemStatement:
Chef received an array AAA of NNN integers as a valentine's day present. He wants to **maximize** the length of the **longest strictly increasing subsequence** by choosing a **subarray** and adding a fixed integer XXX to all its elements.

More formally, Chef wants to **maximize** the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence of AAA by performing the following operation at most once:


Pick 333 integers L,RL, RL,R and X (1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N, X‚ààZ)X \ (1 \leq L \leq R \leq N, \ X \in \mathbb{Z})X (1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N, X‚ààZ) and assign Ai:=Ai+X ‚àÄ i‚àà[L,R]A_i := A_i + X \ \forall \ i \in [L, R]Ai‚Äã:=Ai‚Äã+X ‚àÄ i‚àà[L,R].
Find the length of the longest **strictly** increasing sequence Chef can obtain after performing the operation **at most once**.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN, the size of the array.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers, where the ithi^{th}ith integer denotes AiA_{i}Ai‚Äã.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the length of the longest **strictly** increasing sequence Chef can obtain.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Chef can pick (L,R,X)=(3,3,2)(L,R,X)=(3,3,2)(L,R,X)=(3,3,2). On performing the operation, AAA becomes [1,2,3][1,2,3][1,2,3]. The LIS of the updated array has length 333. The elements of the LIS are [1,2,3][1,2,3][1,2,3].
Test case 222: Chef can pick (L,R,X)=(2,3,‚àí3)(L,R,X)=(2,3,-3)(L,R,X)=(2,3,‚àí3). On performing the operation, AAA becomes [1,2,5,6,9][1,2,5,6,9][1,2,5,6,9]. The LIS of the updated array has length 555. The elements of the LIS are [1,2,5,6,9][1,2,5,6,9][1,2,5,6,9].
Test case 333: Chef can pick (L,R,X)=(4,4,4)(L,R,X)=(4,4,4)(L,R,X)=(4,4,4). On performing the operation, AAA becomes [1,2,2,5][1,2,2,5][1,2,2,5]. The LIS of the updated array has length 333. The elements of the LIS are [1,2,5][1,2,5][1,2,5].
